customer_anxiety_profile: Ronald is low-anxiety and self-directed — he is not a skeptical lead, he is a motivated buyer with a specific technical vision. His risk profile is expectation mismatch, not cold feet. He has been let down by prior ION contact reliability and will notice if the closer is unprepared or generic.
unresolved_concerns: Prior ION reps contacted Ronald multiple times; a callback was promised by 1PM and missed. Ronald was gracious about it but the credibility gap was not fully repaired.

Other companies have only been able to offer 65% of Ronald's estimated electricity offset — he has explicitly stated this is not acceptable. The closer must be prepared to address 100% offset feasibility directly.

Ronald's roof geometry was described as having only 36 feet of usable run, with the north side receiving no direct winter sunlight. Prior companies have cited this as a constraint. The closer needs to address this in the design.
sensitive_topics_raised: Mental health / psychiatric care — Ronald mentioned his psychiatrist in passing (09:15). Tone should remain matter-of-fact and non-patronizing.
Sleep disorder and insomnia — Ronald disclosed he is rarely functional before 1PM (09:56). The 3PM appointment time was chosen specifically for this reason; do not attempt to reschedule earlier.
Remote rural location — Ronald's property is on a private one-lane gravel road; this is a point of pride, not a logistical complaint.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment confirmed for tomorrow (the 5th) at 3PM — phone call with an energy specialist.
Meg's direct line (801 number) provided for any contact needs.
Design team will 'get started right away' on Ronald's options before the appointment.
closer_should_know: Ronald's system expectation is fully off-grid: solar panels, battery storage, power inverters, generator backup, and zero connection to the utility grid ('no connection to the main, whatsoever'). This spec — particularly generator backup integration and full grid disconnection — may exceed Ion's confirmed catalog. The closer must walk in knowing whether Ion can fulfill this spec or must reframe it; Ronald has already rejected every competitor who couldn't meet his 100% offset requirement, and a second disappointment from ION (after the missed callback) will end the deal. Additionally: Ronald self-reported adequate credit and awareness of TPO financing options — credit qualification is not a barrier, but the closer should confirm the specific financing path Ronald is considering.
